title: Cookie Chain — Developer Guide
content:
Cookie Chain — Developer Guide

Cookie Chain is SVM-compatible. Anchor, native Rust, and standard Solana client SDKs work with no changes beyond pointing at the Cookie Chain RPC.

Endpoints:
- HTTP RPC: https://rpc.cookiescan.io
- WebSocket: https://wss.cookiescan.io

JavaScript/TypeScript:
  import { Connection } from '@solana/web3.js';
  const connection = new Connection('https://rpc.cookiescan.io', 'confirmed');

Anchor.toml:
  [provider]
  cluster = 'https://rpc.cookiescan.io'

Deploy a program:
  solana program deploy ./target/deploy/my_program.so

Builder quick path:
- Set CLI/SDK to https://rpc.cookiescan.io
- Deploy programs with Solana CLI, validate on Cookiescan
- Submit ecosystem programs at cookiescan.io/submit-project

Developer tools: cookiescan.io/developer-tools
API playground: api.cookiescan.io/playground

title: Cookiescan Trading API
content:
Cookiescan Trading API

The Cookiescan API provides programmatic access to Cookie Chain blockchain data including transactions, blocks, tokens, validators, and analytics.

API Playground: https://api.cookiescan.io/playground
Base URL: https://api.cookiescan.io

Use the API to build trading bots, indexers, dashboards, and data tools on top of Cookie Chain. The playground lets you explore endpoints interactively.

title: Gorbagana Cash — For Builders
content:
Build On GCASH
For builders
How to migrate or deploy projects depending on whether you are coming from Trashnet or Solana.
Docs
For builders
How to migrate or deploy projects depending on whether you are coming from Trashnet or Solana.
Coming from Trashnet
If you built before the fork, migration should be simple. Your project already targets the same chain environment. In most cases, you only need to replace your old RPC URL with the Gorbagana Cash community RPC.
https://community-rpc.trashscan.io
Coming from Solana
01
Bridge tokens
Bridge a small amount of tokens from Solana to Gorbagana Cash so you can pay for transactions and deployments.
02
Point Solana CLI to GCASH
Configure your Solana CLI to use the Gorbagana Cash RPC endpoint instead of a Solana RPC endpoint.
solana config set --url https://community-rpc.trashscan.io
solana config get
03
Deploy programs
Deploy your existing Solana programs with the CLI. Rust, Anchor, or any compatible Solana program workflow should feel familiar.
solana program deploy ./target/deploy/your_program.so
# Anchor example
anchor deploy --provider.cluster https://community-rpc.trashscan.io
04
Update your dApp RPCs
In your frontend, backend, indexer, or scripts, use Gorbagana Cash RPC endpoints for reads and writes.
Gorbagana Cash RPC
Use this RPC everywhere your Solana app expects a cluster URL.
https://community-rpc.trashscan.io

title: Gorbagana Cash — RPC Endpoints
content:
Network Access
RPC endpoints
Use the community RPC to connect apps, tools, scripts, and Solana-compatible workflows to GCASH.
Docs
RPC endpoints
Use the community RPC to connect apps, tools, scripts, and Solana-compatible workflows to GCASH.
Use the community RPC endpoint when configuring wallets, Solana CLI, dApps, indexers, or backend services.
RPC endpoint: https://community-rpc.trashscan.io
Native token: GCASH

title: Cookie Chain — RPC & Network Endpoints
content:
Cookie Chain — RPC & Network Endpoints

Use these endpoints to connect wallets, CLI tools, dApps, and SDKs to Cookie Chain.

HTTP RPC:  https://rpc.cookiescan.io
WebSocket: https://wss.cookiescan.io

Wallet setup (Nightly or any Solana-compatible wallet):
- Network: Custom SVM
- RPC: https://rpc.cookiescan.io
- WSS: https://wss.cookiescan.io

Solana CLI:
  solana config set --url https://rpc.cookiescan.io

JavaScript/TypeScript:
  new Connection('https://rpc.cookiescan.io', 'confirmed')

Anchor.toml:
  cluster = 'https://rpc.cookiescan.io'
